lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: Fade in. A government building sits by the ocean. A boat approaches the dock.

lines.speaker: Ara Hernandez
lines.text: Oh, hello. I'm sorry. Welcome. I didn't hear you come in.

lines.speaker: Ara Hernandez
lines.text: My name is Ara Hernandez. Are you just arriving from Breeder's Island or are you a foreigner from the land of London?

lines.speaker: Otto
lines.text: Yeah. I just came from Breeder's Island.

lines.speaker: Ara Hernandez
lines.text: Cool. A fellow Westenite. Welcome to the mainland.

lines.speaker: Ara Hernandez
lines.text: You're assigned to the city of West Ward. They're known for their coal mining. And their parties.

lines.speaker: Ara Hernandez
lines.text: Weston is over 1,100 years old. We became one of the strongest countries in the world. Then the Great Xiao-Zunich War happened. It was a dark time.

lines.speaker: Ara Hernandez
lines.text: But now we have a government system for the benefit of the people. And people are happy.

lines.speaker: Ara Hernandez
lines.text: When you turn 25, you enter the Lottery Convention in Draco. Mating or intimate relationships before marriage is illegal and punishable.

lines.speaker: Ara Hernandez
lines.text: Breeder Island-born Westenites and Mainland-born Westenites are all seen as the same. We have to protect each other.

lines.speaker: Ara Hernandez
lines.text: Because without unity there is no future.

lines.speaker: Unknown
lines.text: Otto are you okay? I have to leave. Mom will be back in an hour or two. You stay here.

lines.speaker: Unknown
lines.text: I never scanned my ID when I turned twenty-one. They don't know where I am.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: Soldiers storm the town. Gunfire. People running. A woman falls. Silence.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: Grandfather stands at the door. He looks broken.

lines.speaker: Otto
lines.text: Mom isn't coming back. He went away as well.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: One child starts to cry.

lines.speaker: Otto
lines.text: ...but I'm here.

lines.speaker: Rebecca
lines.text: You're late again.

lines.speaker: Otto
lines.text: I had to stay.

lines.speaker: Rebecca
lines.text: Everyone at school was talking about the tryouts. You're gonna go try out, right?

lines.speaker: Elizabeth
lines.text: You can't give up. I've seen you play. You're so good.

lines.speaker: Otto
lines.text: I don't know yet. But I probably will.

lines.speaker: Rebecca
lines.text: You better. I'm tired of eating rice and beans almost every day.

lines.speaker: Morgan
lines.text: Otto can I talk to you?

lines.speaker: Morgan
lines.text: I'm leaving. I got selected to be a model. I'm going to the capital.

lines.speaker: Morgan
lines.text: I can't wait forever. You've been trying for years. And you're still here.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: She leaves. Otto stays standing.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: Early morning. Otto wakes up, eats hard-boiled eggs, and heads to tryouts.

lines.speaker: Soldier
lines.text: You trying out? ID.

lines.speaker: Soldier
lines.text: You look weak. But you're on the list. Go on.

lines.speaker: Coach Baker
lines.text: Listen up. If you're here to dream — leave now. If you made the team last year, that means nothing.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: Phone notification buzzes. On screen: WESTWARD FC — SELECTED PLAYERS.

lines.speaker: Otto
lines.text: I made it. This feels so unreal.

lines.speaker: Rebecca
lines.text: That's actually you. You made it?

lines.speaker: Elizabeth
lines.text: So it finally happened.

lines.speaker: Rebecca
lines.text: Good. Maybe now we can eat something other than rice and beans.

lines.speaker: Josh
lines.text: Does this mean you're famous?

lines.speaker: Otto
lines.text: No. It just means I have to work even harder.

lines.speaker: Soldier
lines.text: You made it. I was reluctant about you. But congrats. Keep working hard.

lines.speaker: Coach Baker
lines.text: Congratulations. If you're sitting in this room, you made the team.

lines.speaker: Coach Baker
lines.text: Going far in this league means money. Money means survival. For you, your families, and Westward.

lines.speaker: Coach Baker
lines.text: You're not just players. You're hope. Report to training tomorrow morning. Be ready.

lines.speaker: Otto
lines.text: Coach... thank you. For picking me.

lines.speaker: Coach Baker
lines.text: You're depth. Very low on the chart. Don't expect much this season.

lines.speaker: Otto
lines.text: I understand. I'm going to give it my best and surprise you.

title: Match 1 — Westward FC vs Northbridge United

lines.speaker: Reporter
lines.text: Otto, this is your first league match. How are you feeling?

lines.speaker: Otto
lines.text: Ready. I've worked too hard to be nervous.

lines.speaker: Reporter
lines.text: Westward has been struggling. What does this season mean to you?

lines.speaker: Otto
lines.text: Everything. For the team. For West Ward. We're going out there to fight.

lines.speaker: Reporter
lines.text: That was a tough match. What are your thoughts?

lines.speaker: Otto
lines.text: We gave it everything. We keep building from here.

lines.speaker: Coach Baker
lines.text: Good effort out there today. Now we get back to work.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: Win or lose, Otto walks off the pitch knowing this is only the beginning.

lines.speaker: Rebecca
lines.text: I saw the match on TV. You actually played!

lines.speaker: Otto
lines.text: Yeah. And there's more to come.
